body:

Worth a quick conversation?

Andy

subject: Bell / servers + compute for AI workloads
body:
Roweenza,

Bell acquired Distributel in December 2022. We are already an approved vendor there. Wanted to make sure you know us on the Bell side.

I am Andy McLean at OSI Global. We supply Dell and HP servers, plus DIMMs from Samsung, Hynix, and Micron. Your background running Contact Centre AI before moving into the VP Business Technology Services role means you have direct context for what AI compute actually costs. We typically come in 30 to 50% below OEM pricing on servers, and the DDR4 or DDR5 decision alone can be a significant line item depending on the workload.

Worth 15 minutes to compare notes?

subject: Nsight fiber build
body:
Thomas,

Saw Nsight has been pushing fiber into new areas. When carriers are actively building, hardware costs and lead times tend to become a real conversation fast.

We work with carrier IT teams on certified compatible optics and third party maintenance on Cisco and other gear that is running fine but coming off OEM support.

If either of those is live at Nsight, I'd like to learn more about what you're working with.

Worth a quick reply?

subject: RE: Nsight fiber build
body:
Thomas,

Following up. If the timing is off, just say so and I'll leave you alone.

If you're carrying Cisco SmartNet on gear that has been running fine for years, that is typically where we find the most immediate value. We can put a competitive bid together against what you're paying today, no big commitment to do it.

subject: quick follow, Thomas
body:
Thomas,

Different angle. We supply pre owned Cisco and Juniper networking gear, tested, with OSI TPM available. If you're refreshing any segment of the network and want to stretch the budget, it's worth a look.

We also source server memory direct from Samsung and Hynix if DIMM costs are on the refresh agenda, and we're an authorized SmartOptics partner for open line DWDM if that sits in your world.

subject: RE: quick follow, Thomas
body:
Thomas,

I know I've sent a few notes. Not trying to be a pest.

If this isn't the right time, just say so. If hardware costs, TPM, or lead times become a problem at some point down the road, I'd rather be on your radar now than when it's already urgent.

Worth a quick call?

subject: optics for the Armstrong network
body:
Jason,

Saw that Armstrong is acquiring MCTV. 96,000 passings across six states is a serious expansion for the network you are managing.

I'm Andy McLean at OSI Global. We manufacture compatible optics for regional cable and telecom operators in active build and expansion phases. Third party tested, lifetime warranty, ships in 24 to 48 hours.

If you are sourcing transceivers for the current fiber plant or planning ahead for the MCTV integration, I can get samples in front of your team before any pricing conversation.

Worth a look?

subject: RE: optics for the Armstrong network
body:
Jason,

Following up on the note below.

With your background in telecom engineering, you already know what transceiver procurement looks like during a major expansion. OEM lead times and pricing tend to get worse right when volume goes up.

We are a different option. Compatible optics, same performance, fraction of the cost. Samples are free to test.

Is this a conversation worth having?

subject: maintenance costs for the Armstrong fleet
body:
Jason,

Different subject.

As IT Operations Manager, you are likely sitting on Cisco SmartNet or similar OEM maintenance contracts. The Park Place and Service Express merger has put a lot of operators in a tough spot. If you are evaluating alternatives or have contracts coming up for renewal, this is the right moment.

OSI offers TPM across Cisco, Juniper, Arista, Dell, and HP at 40 to 60% below OEM rates. No surprises on pricing and no consolidation risk.

Worth 15 minutes to benchmark what you are paying today?

subject: RE: maintenance costs for the Armstrong fleet
body:
Jason,

Following up on the TPM note.

If contracts are locked in, no problem. One more thing: pre owned networking gear is the other angle most IT Ops managers in your position are pulling right now. Tested Cisco and Juniper, with OSI TPM coverage included. Especially useful when you need to expand the fleet fast without the lead times and price tags that come with new OEM hardware.

With MCTV on the way, it might be worth a conversation before the integration timeline gets tight.

subject: DWDM cost for the Ohio expansion
body:
Mike,

Different angle.

You know the DWDM market better than most. Ciena and Nokia are the defaults, but the pricing and licensing overhead on traditional DWDM platforms are hard to justify when you are scaling fast.

We distribute the SmartOptics DCP platform. Open line system, 30 to 50% below Ciena and Nokia, significantly lower licensing costs, and easier to operate. For a carrier adding route miles and expanding into new markets, the cost difference over the life of the network is substantial.

Worth 20 minutes to compare against what you are running today?

body:
Hi John,

Quick separate note. We do third party maintenance on Cisco, Juniper, Arista, and HP. Worth 10 minutes to compare on whatever your renewal calendar looks like?

subject: altafiber transport options
body:
Hi Stephen,

Reaching out cold. OSI Global supplies optical transport, DWDM, and transceivers to fiber carriers, and I wanted to put us on altafiber's radar.

Two angles people usually find useful: weeks shaved off lead times versus the major OEMs on transceivers and pluggables, and 30 to 50 percent below OEM on DWDM systems via SmartOptics DCP. We are agnostic across Cisco, Juniper, Arista, and Nokia.

Open to a 15 minute intro?
rescheduled: From 2026-06-05 to 2026-06-17 on 2026-05-26 (company-gap-fix, conflict with Sarah Frisbie at altafiber)

subject: RE: altafiber transport options
body:
Wanted to circle back on the optics and DWDM intro. Most of what we ship comes from current Smartoptics batches with manufacturer warranties intact, so it slots in next to your existing footprint without a forklift.

Worth 15 minutes next week to compare a real altafiber line against SmartOptics DCP?

subject: Cisco and Nokia maintenance pricing
body:
Hi Stephen,

Different angle. We also run third party maintenance for Cisco, Juniper, Arista, Nokia, NetApp, Dell, and HP, typically 40 to 60 percent below OEM SmartNet or equivalents. Gartner recognized, privately owned, no PE.

If altafiber has anything in renewal cycles right now, happy to put a head to head quote together.

subject: Server and DIMM pricing for altafiber
body:
Different product line again. We are an authorized Dell and HP partner on new servers, plus we move a lot of Samsung, Hynix, and Micron DIMMs at well below OEM pricing with manufacturer warranties intact.

If altafiber has any server refresh or memory expansion happening this year, we can usually save 20 to 40 percent on the bill of materials. Sample DIMM available if anyone on your team wants to spec test it.

subject: Pre owned Cisco and Juniper inventory
body:
Last angle from me. We also carry pre owned and tested Cisco, Juniper, and Arista networking gear, plus new Nokia as an authorized partner. All gear comes with our own TPM if you want to skip OEM support.

Useful when a project needs a specific SKU yesterday or when a budget cycle is tight. Want me to send our current inventory list?

Caught the move to Stellar President and COO after 22 years at Spartan. Big shift.

We ship SmartOptics DWDM in weeks, not months. Typically 30 to 50 percent below Ciena and Nokia. Most of our regional ISP customers run it on 100G or 400G upgrade paths.

Worth a quick look as the new seat gets going?

Joe,

I work with IT infrastructure leaders at network service providers on two things that usually surface after a deal like this: maintenance contract reviews across the combined gear, and server/component sourcing as you standardize the stack.

Worth a 15-minute conversation?

subject: TPM during the TEC overlay
body:
Hi Ron,

With the TEC close in April and the $500M push for FTTP expansion, vendor support spend across IT and network gear is probably trending up fast. We are Gartner recognized TPM, multi vendor, 40 to 60 percent below OEM on Cisco, Juniper, Arista, NetApp, and Dell.

Worth a 15 minute look at what comes up for renewal next quarter?
pauseReason: Queued via shallow-qualify path that skipped LinkedIn read. Verify still at company before resuming.
resumeReason: VERIFIED 2026-04-26: Path B - The Org + Wiza confirm Director of Information Technology at Vero Networks. Plus 8+ active reposts of Vero job postings on his LinkedIn.

rescheduleHistory.reason: Andy moved E1 from Fri 5/1 to Mon 5/4; rest of sequence shifted +1 biz day.

subject: RE: TPM during the TEC overlay
body:
Cisco bumped SmartNet 8 to 12 percent again this cycle. Same shift on Juniper Care. The pattern usually shows up first at carriers running fast on integrations like the TEC overlay.

If a sample renewal line would help size what shifts at OSI, send me one of the OEM quotes.

subject: Optics for the integrated footprint
body:
Hi Ron,

Different angle. The Vero plus TEC overlay likely produces some new transport spans across the southeast footprint. We private label SmartOptics, ship in days, every part has manufacturer warranty. Pricing on the open line system runs 30 to 50 percent below Ciena and Nokia.

Worth a 15 minute look if any optical buildout is on the table?

---------- Previous message ----------
Subject: RE: TPM during the TEC overlay

Cisco bumped SmartNet 8 to 12 percent again this cycle. Same shift on Juniper Care. The pattern usually shows up first at carriers running fast on integrations like the TEC overlay.

If a sample renewal line would help size what shifts at OSI, send me one of the OEM quotes.

---------- Original message ----------
Subject: TPM during the TEC overlay

Hi Ron,

With the TEC close in April and the $500M push for FTTP expansion, vendor support spend across IT and network gear is probably trending up fast. We are Gartner recognized TPM, multi vendor, 40 to 60 percent below OEM on Cisco, Juniper, Arista, NetApp, and Dell.

Worth a 15 minute look at what comes up for renewal next quarter?

subject: Cisco support at Consolidated Telcom
body:
Joel,

At the IT and Security Manager level at a North Dakota carrier, Cisco SmartNet and other OEM maintenance lines tend to be the quiet line item that grows every renewal cycle.

OSI does third party maintenance on Cisco, Dell, HP, NetApp, Juniper, and Arista at 40 to 60 percent below OEM. Gartner recognized, privately owned, no PE parent. We can keep your gear covered without the OEM rate.

Worth a 15 minute benchmark on what is coming up for renewal in the next two quarters?

body:

Worth a quick call on what your transport roadmap looks like?

body:
Hi Vince,

Your team at Nuveen covers both systems management and architecture planning in one function. Most enterprise shops split those two. That dual ownership usually means the OEM maintenance renewal stack lands on your desk too.

body: Brian, as Nex Tech moves through the TDM to IP transition, the transport layer is usually where the capacity gaps show up first. We build open line DWDM systems at 30 to 50 percent below Ciena and Nokia. Ships in weeks, less rack space, simpler to deploy. Open to walking through what the gap looks like for your network?

subject: server memory pricing for the Nex Tech team
body: Brian, separate from the network side: we supply Samsung and Hynix DIMMs with manufacturer warranties at below OEM pricing. DDR4 is running significantly cheaper than DDR5 for workloads that don't require it. If there's a server refresh tied to the infrastructure modernization, I can put a number together fast.

body: Brian, one more: OSI offers Gartner recognized third party maintenance at 40 to 60 percent below OEM rates, covering Cisco, Juniper, Arista, Dell, and NetApp. Privately owned, no PE pressure on SLAs. If you have gear coming off OEM support in the next 12 months, I can benchmark it fast.
